description_long: This object consists of seven small, sleek airplanes arranged in a tight, arrowhead formation, each leaving a distinct, wispy contrail behind. The airplanes are jet-like, with sharply defined wings and tails, and appear to be performing an aerial maneuver, possibly as part of an airshow or demonstration team. The silhouettes are dark and uniform, suggesting a military or aerobatic team, and the contrails curve gracefully, indicating synchronized movement. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with no visible winglets or dihedral angles. No landing gear or cockpit details are visible, as the focus is on the group formation and dynamic motion. The overall impression is one of precision, speed, and coordinated flight.
description: A group of seven sleek, dark airplanes in tight formation, each trailing a curved contrail, evoking an aerobatic or military display.
description_view_0: Seven airplanes are seen from above in a V-shaped formation, with contrails fanning out behind them.
description_view_1: The airplanes are viewed from the side, highlighting the staggered depth of the formation and the length of the contrails.
description_view_2: A top-down view shows the airplanes in a gentle arc, with contrails curving in parallel.
description_view_3: The formation is seen from a rear oblique angle, emphasizing the spread and convergence of the contrails.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single jet engine per airplane, integrated within the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit; specific seat configuration not visible.
